Yann Sommer.bio: Legendary Swiss goalkeeper who had a storied career at Basel, Borussia Monchengladbach, and Inter Milan, winning the Serie A title and being named Serie A Goalkeeper of the Year. Retired from international football in August 2024 after representing Switzerland at three World Cups and three European Championships.
Yann Sommer.playingStyle: Modern sweeper-keeper with exceptional reflexes, outstanding distribution, and the ability to play as an additional outfield player in possession phases
Yann Sommer.worldCup2026Role: Veteran goalkeeper providing elite experience and mentorship, though his international retirement status may limit his availability unless recalled
Yann Sommer.notableFact: Saved Kylian Mbappe's penalty in the Euro 2020 round of 16 shootout as Switzerland eliminated France in one of the tournament's greatest upsets

Leonidas Stergiou.bio: Young Greek-Swiss center-back who has established himself in the Bundesliga with VfB Stuttgart after progressing through the St. Gallen academy. A composed defender with a mature reading of the game beyond his years.
Leonidas Stergiou.playingStyle: Technically proficient young center-back who is comfortable on the ball and reads the game well, capable of playing in a high defensive line
Leonidas Stergiou.worldCup2026Role: Young center-back option providing depth and future potential behind the established pairing of Akanji and Elvedi or Schar
Leonidas Stergiou.notableFact: Named after the ancient Spartan king Leonidas, he has lived up to his warrior namesake with his fearless defending in the Bundesliga

Silvan Widmer.bio: Experienced right-back who has been a consistent performer for Mainz in the Bundesliga and a regular in the Swiss national team setup for years. Known for his tireless running and reliability on the right flank.
Silvan Widmer.playingStyle: Industrious right-back with excellent stamina, strong crossing ability, and willingness to cover every blade of grass on the right side
Silvan Widmer.worldCup2026Role: Experienced starting right-back or reliable backup who provides width, crossing ability, and defensive solidity on the right flank
Silvan Widmer.notableFact: Has represented Switzerland at multiple major tournaments including Euro 2020, the 2022 World Cup, and Euro 2024, accumulating over 45 caps

Nico Elvedi.bio: Long-serving center-back at Borussia Monchengladbach who has been a pillar of the Swiss defense for years. One of the most consistent Bundesliga defenders of the past decade with over 55 international caps.
Nico Elvedi.playingStyle: Intelligent reading center-back who excels in anticipation, positional play, and winning aerial duels without relying on rash challenges
Nico Elvedi.worldCup2026Role: Established starting center-back alongside Akanji, forming a proven Bundesliga-tested partnership at the heart of Switzerland's defense
Nico Elvedi.notableFact: Has spent his entire Bundesliga career at Borussia Monchengladbach since 2015, becoming one of the club's most loyal and dependable servants

Manuel Akanji.bio: Switzerland's most high-profile defender who has been a key starter at Manchester City, winning multiple Premier League titles under Pep Guardiola. A ball-playing center-back who embodies the modern defender role.
Manuel Akanji.playingStyle: Elite ball-playing center-back trained in Guardiola's system, combining exceptional passing range with strong defensive fundamentals and the ability to carry the ball into midfield
Manuel Akanji.worldCup2026Role: The defensive leader and most important defender in the squad, anchoring the backline and dictating build-up play from the center-back position
Manuel Akanji.notableFact: Has won multiple Premier League titles with Manchester City under Pep Guardiola, becoming one of the most decorated Swiss defenders in history

Remo Freuler.bio: Experienced box-to-box midfielder who has been a dependable presence in the Swiss national team for years. After a long career at Atalanta in Serie A, he moved to Bologna where he has continued to perform at a high level.
Remo Freuler.playingStyle: Reliable box-to-box midfielder with excellent positional sense, tireless running, and the ability to contribute both defensively and offensively
Remo Freuler.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield veteran providing depth and rotation alongside Xhaka, capable of controlling games with his intelligence and work rate
Remo Freuler.notableFact: Has accumulated over 70 caps for Switzerland, making him one of the most experienced players in the current squad alongside Xhaka and Rodriguez

Breel Embolo.bio: Physical and explosive forward of Cameroonian-Swiss heritage who has played across Europe's top leagues. Scored the winner against his birth country Cameroon at the 2022 World Cup and has represented Switzerland at multiple major tournaments.
Breel Embolo.playingStyle: Powerful and direct center-forward who uses his strength and pace to hold up play, run in behind defenses, and finish with conviction
Breel Embolo.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker or primary forward option leading the line with his combination of pace, power, and big-tournament experience
Breel Embolo.notableFact: Scored the only goal against Cameroon, the country of his father's birth, at the 2022 World Cup in a moment laden with personal significance

Michel Aebischer.bio: Versatile Swiss midfielder who has flourished at Bologna in Serie A, playing a key role as the club qualified for the Champions League for the first time. A late bloomer who has established himself as a regular in the national team.
Michel Aebischer.playingStyle: Technically gifted midfielder who can play centrally or on the left, combining creative passing with disciplined defensive work
Michel Aebischer.worldCup2026Role: Versatile midfield option who can play as a creative central midfielder or on the left, providing tactical flexibility in midfield rotations
Michel Aebischer.notableFact: Was instrumental in Bologna's historic Champions League qualification in the 2023-24 season, their first ever appearance in the competition

Noah Okafor.bio: Swiss-Nigerian forward who moved to Leeds United from AC Milan in 2025 after loan spells at Napoli, where he won the Serie A title. A pacy and direct attacker who has represented Switzerland at the 2022 World Cup.
Noah Okafor.playingStyle: Explosive and direct forward who uses his exceptional pace and dribbling to run at defenders, capable of playing across the front line
Noah Okafor.worldCup2026Role: Starting winger or rotational forward option providing pace and directness from the left flank or through the center
Noah Okafor.notableFact: Won the Serie A title with Napoli on loan and then moved to Leeds United for 18 million pounds, establishing himself in the Premier League

Granit Xhaka.bio: Switzerland's captain and most capped outfield player with over 130 international appearances. Revitalized his career at Bayer Leverkusen after a decade at Arsenal, helping the club win their first-ever Bundesliga title with an unbeaten season in 2023-24.
Granit Xhaka.playingStyle: Commanding midfield general who dictates tempo with his passing range, leadership, and positional intelligence, excelling as a deep-lying playmaker or box-to-box midfielder
Granit Xhaka.worldCup2026Role: Captain and midfield conductor who will set the tempo, organize the team, and provide experience across every match as Switzerland's most important player
Granit Xhaka.notableFact: Led Bayer Leverkusen to an historic unbeaten Bundesliga title in 2023-24, transforming his reputation from a divisive Arsenal figure to one of the Bundesliga's best midfielders

Renato Steffen.bio: Experienced Swiss winger who returned to the Swiss Super League with Lugano after spells in the Bundesliga with Wolfsburg and Basel. A versatile attacker who has been a reliable option for the national team across multiple years.
Renato Steffen.playingStyle: Hardworking winger with good crossing ability, tactical discipline, and the experience to perform in different tactical systems
Renato Steffen.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player providing depth on the wing and versatility across attacking positions from the bench
Renato Steffen.notableFact: Won four consecutive Swiss Super League titles with Basel early in his career before earning a move to Wolfsburg in the Bundesliga

Gregor Kobel.bio: One of the most highly rated goalkeepers in the Bundesliga, starring for Borussia Dortmund with a market value reflecting his elite status. Despite limited international caps, he is widely regarded as Switzerland's future number one.
Gregor Kobel.playingStyle: Commanding modern goalkeeper with outstanding shot-stopping, excellent reflexes, and confident authority in the penalty area
Gregor Kobel.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper or primary competitor for the number one spot, bringing elite Bundesliga and Champions League experience between the posts
Gregor Kobel.notableFact: Was Borussia Dortmund's starting goalkeeper in the 2024 Champions League final against Real Madrid, showcasing his ability on the biggest stage

Ricardo Rodríguez.bio: Switzerland's most capped active player with over 110 international appearances spanning more than a decade. The veteran left-back has played at three World Cups and three European Championships across a career that included AC Milan, Wolfsburg, Torino, and now Real Betis.
Ricardo Rodríguez.playingStyle: Technically excellent left-back renowned for his crossing accuracy, dead-ball delivery, and ability to contribute offensively while maintaining defensive discipline
Ricardo Rodríguez.worldCup2026Role: Veteran starting left-back bringing unmatched tournament experience and reliable performances, or elder statesman providing guidance and depth if younger options are preferred
Ricardo Rodríguez.notableFact: Has represented Switzerland at six major tournaments (three World Cups, three Euros), making him one of the most tournament-experienced players in world football

Steven Zuber.bio: Experienced attacking player who has operated across multiple positions throughout his career in Germany, the USA, and Greece. A versatile option who has contributed key moments in major tournaments for Switzerland.
Steven Zuber.playingStyle: Versatile attacker who can play as a winger or wing-back, combining technical ability with intelligent positioning and an eye for assists
Steven Zuber.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player providing tactical flexibility and the ability to play in multiple attacking positions when needed
Steven Zuber.notableFact: Scored a crucial equalizer against Brazil at the 2018 World Cup and has provided key contributions across multiple major tournaments for Switzerland

Djibril Sow.bio: Athletic box-to-box midfielder who joined Sevilla in La Liga in 2023 after a successful spell at Eintracht Frankfurt where he won the Europa League. A powerful presence in midfield who combines physicality with technical quality.
Djibril Sow.playingStyle: Athletic and powerful box-to-box midfielder who covers enormous ground, wins possession through physicality, and drives forward with the ball
Djibril Sow.worldCup2026Role: Starting midfielder or key rotation option alongside Xhaka, providing the energy, physicality, and ball-carrying ability to complement the captain's passing game
Djibril Sow.notableFact: Won the Europa League with Eintracht Frankfurt in 2022, scoring in the semifinal against West Ham on the way to the title

Dan Ndoye.bio: Breakout star of Euro 2024 who electrified the tournament with his blistering pace and dribbling for Switzerland. The Swiss-Senegalese winger recorded a top speed of 35.6 km/h at the Euros, faster than Kylian Mbappe.
Dan Ndoye.playingStyle: Explosive pace merchant who terrorizes defenses with his speed and dribbling, capable of playing as a winger or wing-back with devastating effect
Dan Ndoye.worldCup2026Role: Starting winger or wing-back whose raw pace and direct running will be Switzerland's primary weapon in transition and counter-attacks
Dan Ndoye.notableFact: Recorded a top speed of 35.6 km/h at Euro 2024, making him the third-fastest player at the tournament, quicker than Kylian Mbappe

Ruben Vargas.bio: Swiss winger of Dominican heritage who became the first Bundesliga player of Dominican descent when he joined Augsburg in 2019. After 161 games for Augsburg, he moved to Sevilla in La Liga, continuing to develop his game in a top European league.
Ruben Vargas.playingStyle: Diminutive and tricky winger with a low center of gravity, quick feet, and the ability to beat defenders in tight spaces reminiscent of Xherdan Shaqiri
Ruben Vargas.worldCup2026Role: Rotational winger option providing creativity and unpredictability from the flanks, capable of unlocking defenses with individual skill
Ruben Vargas.notableFact: Became the first player of Dominican descent to play in the Bundesliga when he joined Augsburg, breaking new ground for Caribbean representation in German football

Vincent Sierro.bio: Central midfielder playing for Toulouse in Ligue 1, providing a steady and reliable midfield presence. A product of the Sion academy who has developed into a dependable option at both club and international level.
Vincent Sierro.playingStyle: Steady and composed central midfielder who keeps possession ticking over, provides simple but effective distribution, and maintains the team's shape
Vincent Sierro.worldCup2026Role: Midfield depth option capable of coming off the bench to maintain possession and control in the center of the park
Vincent Sierro.notableFact: A product of FC Sion's academy in the Swiss Super League before earning a move to Ligue 1 football with Toulouse

Cedric Itten.bio: Target-man striker who has played for Young Boys in the Swiss Super League after spells at Rangers, Greuther Furth, and Stuttgart. A tall and physical forward offering a different dimension to Switzerland's attacking options.
Cedric Itten.playingStyle: Traditional target forward who uses his height and strength to win aerial duels, hold up play, and bring others into the game
Cedric Itten.worldCup2026Role: Alternative striking option from the bench when Switzerland needs to change their approach to a more direct, physical style of attack
Cedric Itten.notableFact: Had a spell at Rangers in Scottish football where he experienced European competition before returning to develop in the Bundesliga and Swiss leagues

Zeki Amdouni.bio: Exciting young Swiss-Tunisian striker who has risen rapidly through the ranks, signing for Burnley before a productive loan at Benfica in Portugal. Scored six goals in Euro 2024 qualifying to announce himself on the international stage.
Zeki Amdouni.playingStyle: Mobile and clinical striker who combines intelligent movement with sharp finishing, capable of dropping deep to link play or making runs in behind
keyStrengths: Ability to drop deep and link play
Zeki Amdouni.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker or impactful substitute providing goalscoring threat and intelligent movement in the final third alongside or instead of Embolo
Zeki Amdouni.notableFact: Scored six goals during Euro 2024 qualifying, the most of any Swiss player, announcing himself as a key figure for the national team's future

Philipp Köhn.bio: Young goalkeeper who has developed at Monaco in Ligue 1, competing for playing time at one of France's top clubs. A German-born keeper who represents Switzerland at international level.
Philipp Köhn.playingStyle: Agile and technically sound goalkeeper with good reflexes and developing command of his penalty area
Philipp Köhn.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing squad depth and gaining valuable tournament experience alongside Kobel and Sommer
Philipp Köhn.notableFact: Has been competing for the starting goalkeeper position at Monaco in Ligue 1, gaining exposure in European competition alongside teammate Denis Zakaria

Fabian Schär.bio: Veteran center-back who has been a stalwart at Newcastle United since 2018, known for his powerful free kicks and long-range shooting. Played at three World Cups and three European Championships before retiring from international football in 2024.
Fabian Schär.playingStyle: Ball-striking center-back with a powerful right foot capable of scoring spectacular goals from distance, combining traditional defensive strength with offensive threat from set pieces
Fabian Schär.worldCup2026Role: If recalled from international retirement, a veteran defensive leader and goal-threat from set pieces providing invaluable experience at the back
Fabian Schär.notableFact: Won the 2025 EFL Cup with Newcastle United, the club's first domestic trophy in 70 years, and has scored 8 international goals as a center-back

Fabian Rieder.bio: Young creative midfielder who moved to Rennes in Ligue 1 after developing at Young Boys in the Swiss Super League. A technically gifted playmaker who has quickly established himself in the Swiss national team setup.
Fabian Rieder.playingStyle: Creative and technically gifted attacking midfielder with excellent vision, precise passing, and the ability to unlock defenses with through balls
Fabian Rieder.worldCup2026Role: Creative attacking option who can play as a right winger or attacking midfielder, providing the flair and invention to break down organized defenses
Fabian Rieder.notableFact: Made his breakthrough at Young Boys before earning a move to Ligue 1, rapidly becoming one of Switzerland's most exciting young attacking talents

Denis Zakaria.bio: Powerful defensive midfielder and Monaco captain who has played at the highest level including spells at Juventus, Chelsea, and Borussia Monchengladbach. A physically imposing midfielder who combines athleticism with technical quality.
Denis Zakaria.playingStyle: Physically dominant defensive midfielder who wins possession through strength and timing, capable of driving forward with the ball and scoring spectacular goals
Denis Zakaria.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield enforcer providing defensive solidity and physical presence alongside Xhaka, breaking up opposition attacks and launching transitions
Denis Zakaria.notableFact: Was named Monaco captain by manager Adi Hutter and has played for some of Europe's biggest clubs including Juventus and Chelsea before finding his best form at Monaco

Ardon Jashari.bio: Emerging Swiss-Kosovar midfielder who became AC Milan's latest signing in August 2025 after an outstanding season at Club Brugge where he won Player of the Year and Young Player of the Year in the Belgian Pro League.
Ardon Jashari.playingStyle: Versatile and combative midfielder who excels as both a defensive midfielder and box-to-box player, reading the game excellently and winning possession cleanly
Ardon Jashari.worldCup2026Role: Rising midfield talent providing dynamic depth and the ability to play both defensive and box-to-box roles, representing Switzerland's next generation of midfielders
Ardon Jashari.notableFact: Won the 2025 Belgian Golden Boot award and was voted both Player of the Year and Young Player of the Year in the Belgian Pro League at Club Brugge before joining AC Milan

Eray Cömert.bio: Swiss-Turkish center-back who plays for Valencia in La Liga after coming through the Basel academy. A composed defender who has gained experience across multiple European leagues including spells in Germany and Spain.
Eray Cömert.playingStyle: Technically secure center-back who is comfortable playing out from the back and maintaining a high defensive line, with good one-on-one defending ability
Eray Cömert.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option at center-back who can step in when needed, bringing La Liga experience and tactical awareness to the squad
Eray Cömert.notableFact: Came through the highly regarded FC Basel academy, the same institution that developed other Swiss internationals including Fabian Schar and Granit Xhaka
